author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was right indeed, most beloved, that we should rather hear our Brother, my colleague, when present before all of us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And just now he refused not, but put us off; for he extorted from me that he might now listen to me, on the condition that I also may listen to him, for in charity itself we are all listening unto Him, who is our One Master in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend therefore to the Psalm, entitled A Song of Degrees; considerably longer than the rest under the same title.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not therefore linger, save where necessity shall compel us: that we may, if the Lord permit, explain the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye also ought not to hear everything as men untaught; ye ought in some degree to aid us from your past listenings, so that it may not be needful that everything should be declared to you as though new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, remember David, and all his meekness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David according to the truth of history was one man, king of Israel, son of Jesse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was indeed meek, as the Divine Scriptures themselves mark and command him, and so meek that he did not even render evil for evil to his persecutor Saul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He preserved towards him so great humility, that he acknowledged him a king, and himself a dog: and answered the king not proudly nor rudely, though he was more powerful in God; but he rather endeavoured to appease him by humility, than to provoke him by pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saul was even given into his power, and this by the Lord God, that he might do to him what he listed: but since he was not commanded to slay him, but had it only placed in his power (now a man is permitted to use his power), he rather turned towards mercy what God gave him.…The humility of David is therefore commended, the meekness of David is commended; and it is said to God, “Lord, remember David, and all his meekness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How he sware unto the Lord, and vowed a vow unto the Almighty God of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore remember for this, that he may fulfil what he hath promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David himself vowed as though he had it in his power, and he prayeth God to fulfil his vow: there is devotion in the vow, but there is humility in the prayer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one presume to think he fulfilled by his own strength what he hath vowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who exhorteth thee to vow, Himself aideth thee to fulfil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore see what he vowed, and hence we comprehend how David should be understood in a figure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “David” is interpreted, “Strong of hand,” for he was a great warrior.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trusting indeed in the Lord his God, he despatched all wars, he laid low all his enemies, God helping him, according to the dispensation of that kingdom; prefiguring nevertheless some One strong of hand to destroy His enemies, the devil and his angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These enemies the Church warreth against, and conquereth.…What then doth he mean, “How he sware,” etc.?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see what vow is this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We can offer God nothing more pleasing than to swear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now to swear is to promise firmly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider this vow, that is, with what ardour he vowed what he vowed, with what love, with what longing; nevertheless, he prayeth the Lord to fulfil it in these words, “O Lord, remember David, and all his meekness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this temper he vowed his vow, and there should be a house of God: “I will not come within the tabernacle of mine house, nor climb up into my bed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not suffer mine eyes to sleep, nor mine eyelids to slumber” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This seemeth not enough; he adds, “Neither the temples of my head to take any rest, until I find out a place for the Lord; an habitation for the God of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where did he seek a place for the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he was meek, he sought it in himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how is one a place for the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Prophet: “Upon whom shall My Spirit rest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even upon him that is poor and of a contrite spirit, and trembleth at My words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to be a place for the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou poor in spirit, and contrite, and trembling at the word of God, and thou wilt thyself be made what thou seekest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if what thou seekest be not realized in thyself, what doth it profit thee in another.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many thousands believed, my brethren, when they laid down the price of their possessions at the Apostles’ feet!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what saith Scripture of them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely they are become a temple of God; not only each respectively a temple of God, but also all a temple of God together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have therefore become a place for the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that ye may know that one place is made for the Lord in all, Scripture saith, They were of one heart and one soul toward God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But many, so as not to make a place for the Lord, seek their own things, love their own things, delight in their own power, are greedy for their private interests.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereas he who wisheth to make a place for the Lord, should rejoice not in his private, but the common good.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore, brethren, abstain from the possession of private property; or from the love of it, if we may not from its possession; and we make a place for the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is too much for me, saith some one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider who thou art, who art about to make a place for the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If any senator wished to be entertained at your house, I say not senator, the deputy of some great man of this world, and should say, something offends me in thy house; though thou shouldest love it, thou wouldest remove it, nevertheless, lest thou shouldest offend him, whose friendship thou wast courting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what doth man’s friendship profit thee?…Desire the friendship of Christ without fear: He wishes to be entertained at thy house; make room for Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, make room for Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love not thyself, love Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou love thyself, thou shuttest the door against Him; if thou love Him, thou openest unto Him: and if thou open and He enter, thou shalt not be lost by loving thyself, but shalt find thyself with Him who loveth thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lo, we heard of the same at Ephrata” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A place for the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We heard of it at Ephrata: and found it in the plains of the forests.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did he hear it where he found it? or did he hear it in one place, find it in another?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore enquire what Ephrata is, where he heard it; let us also enquire what mean the plains of the forests, where he found it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ephrata, a Hebrew word, is rendered in Latin by Speculum, as the translators of Hebrew words in the Scriptures have handed down to us, that we might understand them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have translated from Hebrew into Greek, and from Greek we have versions into Latin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there have been who watched in the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore Ephrata meaneth a mirror, that house which was found in the woodland plains, was heard of in a mirror.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A mirror hath an image: all prophecy is an image of things future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The future house of God, therefore, was declared in the image of prophecy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We have found it in the plains of the forests.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the “plains of the forests”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saltus is not here used in its common sense, as a plot of ground of so many hundred acres; saltus properly signifies a spot as yet untilled and woody.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some copies read, in the plains of the wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then were the woodland plains, save nations yet untilled? what were they, save regions yet covered with the thorns of idolatry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus, though there were thorns of idolatry there, still we find a place for the Lord there, a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was declared in the image to the Jews, was manifested in the faith of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We will go into His tabernacles” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those of the Lord God of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who enter to dwell therein, are the very same who enter that they may be dwelt in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou enterest into thy house, that thou mayest dwell therein; into the house of God, that thou mayest be dwelt in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord is better, and when He hath begun to dwell in thee, He will make thee happy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou be not dwelt in by Him, thou wilt be miserable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That son who said, “Father, give me the portion of the goods,” etc., wished to be his own master.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was well kept in his father’s hands, that it might not be wasted with harlots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He received it, it was given into his own power; going to a far country, he squandered it all with harlots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At length he suffered hunger, he remembered his father; he returned, that he might be satisfied with bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Enter therefore, that thou mayest be dwelt in; and mayest be not thine own, so to speak, but His: “We will go into His tabernacles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will worship on the spot where His feet stood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord’s, or those of the house of the Lord itself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is the Lord’s house, wherein he saith He ought to be worshipped.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beside His house, the Lord heareth not unto eternal life; for he belongeth to God’s house, who hath in charity been built in with living stones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who hath not charity, falleth; and while he falls, the house stands.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if ye incline to understand it of the house itself, where the feet of that house have stood; let thy feet stand in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They will then stand, if thou shalt persevere in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is said of the devil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He was a murderer from the beginning, and stood not in the truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The feet of the devil therefore stood not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Also what saith he of the proud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O let not the foot of pride come against me; and let not the hand of the ungodly cast me down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are they fallen, all that work wickedness: they are cast down, and were not able to stand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That then is the house of God, whose feet stand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence John rejoicing, saith: what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom standeth and heareth him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he stand not, he heareth him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly he standeth, because “he rejoiceth on account of the bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore ye see why they fell, who rejoice because of their own voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That friend of the Bridegroom said, “The same is He which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some say, We baptize: rejoicing in their own voice, they could not stand; and belong not to that house of which it is said, “where His feet stood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, into Thy resting place” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith unto the Lord sleeping, “Arise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know already who slept, and who rose again.…“Thou, and the ark of Thy sanctification:” that is, Arise, that the ark of Thy sanctification, which Thou hast sanctified, may arise also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is our Head; His ark is His Church: He arose first, the Church will arise also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The body would not dare to promise itself resurrection, save the Head arose first.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Body of Christ, that was born of Mary, hath been understood by some to be the ark of sanctification; so that the words mean, Arise with Thy Body, that they who believe not may handle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Thy priests be clothed with righteousness, and let Thy saints sing with joyfulness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When Thou risest from the dead, and goest unto Thy Father, let that royal Priesthood be clothed with faith, since “the righteous liveth by faith;” and, receiving the pledge of the Holy Spirit, let the members rejoice in the hope of resurrection, which went before in the Head: for to them the Apostle saith, “Rejoicing in hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy servant David’s sake, turn not away the face of Thine Anointed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words are addressed unto God the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy servant David’s sake, turn not away the face of Thine Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord was crucified in Judæa; He was crucified by the Jews; harassed by them, He slept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He arose to judge those among whose savage hands He slept: and He saith elsewhere, “Raise Thou Me up again, and I shall reward them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He both hath rewarded them, and will reward them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews well know themselves how great were their sufferings after the Lord’s death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were all expelled from the very city, where they slew Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then? have all perished even from the root of David and from the tribe of Judah?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: for some of that stock believed, and in fact many thousands of men of that stock believed, and this after the Lord’s resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They raged and crucified Him: and afterwards began to see miracles wrought in the Name of Him Crucified; and they trembled still more that His Name should have so much power, since when in their hands He seemed unable to work any; and pricked at heart, at length believing that there was some hidden divinity in Him whom they had believed like other men, and asking counsel of the Apostles, they were answered, “Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the Name of our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then Christ arose to judge those by whom He had been crucified, and turned away His Presence from the Jews, turning His Presence towards the Gentiles; God is, as it seemeth, besought in behalf of the remnant of Israel; and it is said unto Him, “For Thy servant David’s sake, turn not away the presence of Thine Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the chaff be condemned, let the wheat be gathered together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May the remnant be saved, as Isaiah saith, “And the remnant hath” clearly “been saved:” for out of them were the twelve Apostles, out of them more than five hundred brethren, to whom the Lord showed Himself after His Resurrection: out of their number were so many thousands baptized, who laid the price of their possessions at the Apostles’ feet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then was fulfilled the prayer here made to God: “For Thy servant David’s sake, turn not away the presence of Thine Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath made a faithful oath unto David, and He shall not repent” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “hath made an oath”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath confirmed a promise through Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “He shall not repent”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will not change.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God suffereth not the pain of repentance, nor is He deceived in any matter, so that He would wish to correct that wherein He hath erred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as when a man repents of anything, he wisheth to change what he hath done; thus where thou hearest that God repenteth, look for an actual change.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God doth it differently from thee, although He calleth it by the name of repentance; for thou dost it, because thou hadst erred; while He doth it, because He avengeth, or freeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He changed Saul’s kingdom, when He repented, as it is said: and in the very passage where the Scripture saith, “It repented Him;” it is said a little after, “for He is not a man that He should repent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore He changeth His works through His immutable counsel, He is said to repent on account of this very change, not of His counsel, but of His work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He promised this so as not to change it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as this passage also saith: “The Lord sware, and will not repent, Thou art a Priest for ever after the order of Melchizedec;” so also since this was promised so that it should not be changed, because it must needs happen and be permanent; he saith, “The Lord hath made a faithful oath unto David, and He shall not repent; Of the fruit of thy body shall I set upon thy seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He might have said, “of the fruit of thy loins,” wherefore did He choose to say, “Of the fruit of thy body”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had He said that also, it would have been true; but He chose to say with a further meaning, Ex fructu ventris, because Christ was born of a woman without the man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath made a faithful oath unto David, and He shall not shrink from it; Of the fruit of thy body shall I set upon thy seat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thy children will keep My covenant and My testimonies that I shall learn them, their children also shall sit upon thy seat for evermore” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thy children keep My covenant, their children also shall sit for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The parents establish a desert on behalf of their children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if his children should keep the covenant, and their children should not keep it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why is the happiness of the children promised in relation to their parents’ deservings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what saith He, “If thy children will keep My covenant, their children also shall sit for evermore”—He saith not, if thy children keep My covenant, they shall sit upon thy seat; and if their children keep My covenant, they also shall sit upon thy seat: but he saith, “If thy children keep My covenant, their children also shall sit upon thy seat for evermore”—except because He here wished their fruit to be understood by their children?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If thy children,” He saith, “will keep My covenant, and if thy children shall keep My testimonies that I shall learn them; their children also shall sit upon thy seat:” that is, this will be their fruit, that they sit upon thy seat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in this life, brethren, do all of us who labour in Christ, all of us who tremble at His words, who in any way endeavour to execute His will, and groan while we pray His help that we may fulfil what He commandeth; do we already sit in those seats of bliss which are promised us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: but holding His commandments, we hope this will come to pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hope is spoken of under the figure of sons; because sons are the hope of man living in this life, sons are his fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason also men, when excusing their avarice, allege that they are reserving for their children what they hoard up; and, unwilling to give to the destitute, excuse themselves under the name of piety, because their children are their hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all men who live according to this world, declare it to be their hope, to be fathers of children they may leave behind them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then He describes hope generally under the name of children, and saith, “If thy children will keep My covenant and My testimonies that I shall learn them, their children also shall sit upon thy seat for evermore:” that is, they shall have such fruits, that their hope shall not deceive them, that they may come there where they hope to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At present therefore they are as fathers, men of hope for the future; but when they have attained what they hope, they are children; because they have brought forth and produced in their works that which they gain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is preserved unto them for the future, because futurity itself commonly signifieth children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or if thou understand actual men to be meant by children, the words, “If thy children will keep My covenant and My testimonies that I shall teach them,” may mean, “If thy children will keep My covenant and testimonies that I shall teach them, and their children also;” that is, if they too keep My covenant; so that here thou must make a slight pause, and then infer that “they shall sit upon thy seat for evermore;” that is, both thy children and their children, but all if they keep My covenant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, if they keep it not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath the promise of God failed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: but it is said and promised for this reason, that God foresaw: what, save that they would believe?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that no man should as it were threaten God’s promises, and prefer to place in his own power the fulfilment of what God promised: for this reason he saith, “He made an oath:” whereby he showeth that it will without doubt take place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then hath He said here, “If they will keep My covenant”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Glory not in the promises, and leave out thy failing to keep the covenant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then wilt thou be the son of David, if thou shalt keep the covenant; but if thou dost not keep it, thou wilt not be David’s son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God promised to the sons of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say not, I am David’s son if thou degenerate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the Jews, who were born of this very stock, say not this (nay, they say it, but they are under a delusion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord saith openly, “If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He thereby denied them to be children, because they did not the works), how do we call ourselves David’s children, who are not of his race according to the flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It follows then that we are not children, save by imitating his faith, save by worshipping God, as he worshipped.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore what thou hopest not through descent, thou wilt not endeavour to obtain by works; how shall the sitting upon David’s seat be fulfilled in thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if it shall not be fulfilled in thee, thinkest thou that it shall not be fulfilled at all?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how hath He found it in the woodland tracts? and how did His feet stand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever then thou mayest be, that house will stand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord hath chosen Sion to be an habitation for Himself” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sion is the Church Herself; She is also that Jerusalem unto whose peace we are running, who is in pilgrimage not in the Angels, but in us, who in her better part waiteth for the part that will return; whence letters have come unto us, which are every day read.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This city is that very Sion, whom the Lord hath chosen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This shall be My rest for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the words of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My rest:” I rest there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How greatly doth God love us, brethren, since, because we rest, He saith that He also resteth!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is not sometimes Himself disturbed, nor doth He rest as we do; but He saith that He resteth there, because we shall have rest in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Here will I dwell: for I have a delight therein.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will bless her widow with blessings, and will satisfy her poor with bread” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every soul that is aware that it is bereft of all help, save of God alone, is widowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how doth the Apostle describe a widow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “She that is a widow indeed and desolate, trusteth in God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was speaking of those whom we all call Widows in the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith, “She that liveth in pleasure, is dead while she liveth;” and he numbereth her not among the widows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in describing true widows, what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “She that is a widow indeed and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here he addeth, “but she that liveth in pleasure, is dead while she liveth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then makes a widow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That she hath no aid from any other source, save from God alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that have husbands, take pride in the protection of their husbands: widows seem desolate, and their aid is a stronger one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole Church therefore is one widow, whether in men or in women, in married men or married women, in young men or in old, or in virgins: the whole Church is one widow, desolate in this world, if she feel this, if she is aware of her widowhood: for then is help at hand for her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye not recognise this widow in the Gospel, my brethren, when the Lord declared “that men ought always to pray and not to faint”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There was in a city a judge,” He said, “which feared not God, neither regarded man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him day by day, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The widow, by daily importunity, prevailed with him: for the judge said within himself, “Though I fear not God; neither regard man, yet because this woman troubleth me, I will avenge her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the wicked judge heard the widow, that he might not be molested; heareth not God His Church, whom He exhorteth to pray?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Also, “I will satisfy her poor with bread;” what meaneth this, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be poor, and we shall then be satisfied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many who trust in the world, and are proud, are Christians; they worship Christ, but are not satisfied; for they have been satisfied, and abound in their pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of such it is said, “Our soul is filled with the scornful reproof of the wealthy, and with the despitefulness of the proud:” these have abundance, and therefore eat, but are not satisfied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is said of them in the Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All such as be fat upon the earth have eaten and worshipped.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They worship Christ, they venerate Christ, they pray unto Christ; but they are not satisfied with His wisdom and righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they are not poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the poor, that is the humble in heart, the more they hunger, the more they eat; and the more empty they are of the world, the more hungry they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is full refuseth whatsoever thou wilt give him, because he is full.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give me one who hungereth; give me one of whom it is said, “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled:” and these will be the poor of whom he hath just said, “And will satisfy her poor with bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the very Psalm where it is said, “All such as be fat upon the earth have eaten and worshipped;” this is said of the poor also, and exactly in the same manner as in this Psalm, “The poor shall eat, and be satisfied: they that seek after the Lord shall praise Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where it is said, “All such as be fat upon earth have eaten and worshipped:” it is said, “the poor shall eat, and be satisfied.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, when the rich are said to have worshipped, are they not said to be satisfied; yet when the poor are mentioned, they are said to be satisfied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence are they satisfied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the nature, brethren, of this satisfying?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God Himself is their bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bread came down upon the earth, that He might become milk unto us; and said to His own, “I am the Living Bread which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence these words in the Psalm, “The poor shall eat, and be satisfied.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From what source shall they be satisfied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what followeth: “And they that seek after the Lord shall praise Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be ye therefore poor, be ye among the members of that widow, let your help be solely in God alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Money is nought; not thence will ye have aid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many have been cast headlong down for money’s sake, many have perished on account of money; many for the sake of their riches have been marked out by plunderers; they would have been safe, had they not had what made men hunt for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many have presumed in their more powerful friends: they in whom they presumed have fallen, and have involved in their ruin those who trusted in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look back upon the instances to be seen in the human race.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it anything singular that I am telling you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We speak these things not only from these Scriptures; read them in the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take heed that ye presume not in money, in a friend, in the honour and the boasting of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take away all these things: but if thou hast them, thank God if thou despisest them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou art puffed up by them; think not when thou wilt be the prey of men; already art thou the Devil’s prey.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou hast not trusted in these things, thou wilt be among the members of that widow, who is the Church, of whom it is said, “I will bless her widow with blessings;” thou wilt also be poor, and one of those of whom it is said, “And will satisfy her poor with bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes, however, and we must not pass over this without mention, thou findest a poor man proud, and a rich man humble: we daily endure such persons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest a poor man groaning beneath a rich man, and when the more powerful rich man presseth upon him, then thou seest him humble: sometimes not even then, but even then proud; whence thou seest what he would have been, had he any property.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God’s poor one is therefore poor in spirit, not in his purse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes a man goeth forth having a full house, rich lands, many estates, much gold and silver; he knoweth that he must not trust in these, he humbleth himself before God, he doth good with them; thus his heart is raised unto God, so that he is aware that not only do riches themselves profit him nothing, but that they even impede his feet, save He rule them, and aid them: and he is counted among the poor who are satisfied with bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou findest another a proud beggar, or not proud only because he hath nothing, nevertheless seeking whereby he may be puffed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God doth not heed the means a man hath, but the wish he hath, and judgeth him according to his wish for temporal blessings, not according to the means which it is not his lot to have.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Apostle saith of the rich, “Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore should they do with their riches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goeth on to say: “That they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And see that they are poor in this world: “Laying up in store for themselves,” he addeth, “a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they have laid hold of eternal life, then will they be rich; but since they have it not as yet, they should know that they are poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus it is that God counteth among His poor all the humble in heart, who are established in that twofold charity, whatever they may have in this world—among His poor, whom He satisfieth with bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will clothe her priests with salvation, and her saints shall rejoice and sing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are now at the end of the Psalm; attend for a short space, Beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will clothe her priests with salvation, and her saints shall rejoice and sing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is our salvation, save our Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, therefore, “I will clothe her priests with salvation”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As many of you as have been baptized into Christ, have put on Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And her saints shall rejoice and sing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence shall they rejoice and sing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they have been clothed with salvation: not in themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they have become light, but in the Lord; for they were darkness before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he hath added, “There will I raise up the horn of David” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17): this will be David’s height, that trust be put in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For horn signifieth height: and what sort of height?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, while all the bones are wrapped up in flesh, the horn goeth beyond the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Spiritual altitude is a horn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is spiritual loftiness, save to trust in Christ? not to say, It is my work, I baptize; but, “He it is who baptizeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is the horn of David: and that ye may know that there is the horn of David, heed what followeth: “I have ordained a lantern for mine Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is a lantern?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye already know the Lord’s words concerning John: “He was a burning and a shining light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith John?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He it is who baptizeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Herein therefore shall the saints rejoice, herein the priests shall rejoice: because all that is good in themselves, is not of themselves, but of Him who hath the power of baptizing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fearlessly therefore doth every one who hath received baptism come unto His temple; because it is not man’s, but His who made the horn of David to flourish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon Him shall My sanctification flourish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Upon Mine Anointed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when He saith, “Mine anointed,” it is the voice of the Father, who saith, “I will bless her widow with blessings, and will satisfy her poor with bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will clothe her priests with salvation, and her saints shall rejoice and sing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who saith, “There will I raise up the horn of David,” is God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He Himself saith, “I have ordained a lantern for Mine Anointed,” because Christ is both ours and the Father’s: He is our Christ, when He saveth us and ruleth us, as He is also our Lord: He is the Son of the Father, but both our Christ and the Father’s.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He were not the Father’s Christ, it would not be said above, “For Thy servant David’s sake, turn not Thou away the presence of Thine Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon Him shall My sanctification flourish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It flourisheth upon Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none of men assume this to himself, that he himself sanctifieth: otherwise it will not be true, “Upon Him shall My sanctification flourish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The glory of sanctification shall flourish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sanctification of Christ therefore in Christ Himself, is the power of the sanctification of God in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that he saith, “shall flourish,” he refers to His glory: for when trees flourish, then are they beautiful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sanctification therefore is in Baptism: thence it flourisheth, and is brightened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hath the world yielded to this beauty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it flourisheth in Christ; for, put it in man’s power, and how doth it then flourish? since “all flesh in grass, and all the goodliness thereof as the flower of the grass.”
